title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the kth largest element in the array. Note that it is the kth largest, not the kth distinct element.
hints: Sorting gives O(n log n). Can you do better?
A min-heap of size k always has the kth largest element at the top.
Push elements into a min-heap. When size exceeds k, pop the smallest. After processing all elements, the top is the kth largest.
follow_ups: Can you solve it in O(n) average time with quickselect?
What if the array is a stream?

title: Find Median from Data Stream
description: Design a data structure that supports addNum(num) to add an integer from the stream and findMedian() to return the median of all elements added so far.
hints: The median splits data into two halves. What if you could efficiently access the largest of the small half and smallest of the large half?
Use two heaps: a max-heap for the lower half and a min-heap for the upper half.
Keep both heaps balanced in size (differ by at most 1). Median is either the top of the larger heap or average of both tops.
follow_ups: What if you need to remove elements too?
Can you handle it if 99% of numbers are between 0 and 100?

title: Merge K Sorted Lists
description: You are given an array of k linked lists, each sorted in ascending order. Merge all the linked lists into one sorted linked list and return it.
hints: You need to repeatedly find the smallest element among k list heads. What's efficient for that?
A min-heap of size k lets you extract the minimum in O(log k).
Push the head of each list into a min-heap. Pop the minimum, add to result, and push that node's next (if exists) into the heap.
follow_ups: Can you solve it with divide and conquer?
What if the lists are very long but k is small?
